parametrized_description: A village delivers mail to nearby villages either by runners or canoeers. Runners can carry V bags of mail each trip and take U hours. Canoeers can carry Z bags of mail each trip and take N hours. At most P of deliveries can be made by canoe. Additionally, the village can spare at most C total hours for deliveries and must use at least E runners. The objective is to determine the number of trips by runners and canoeers to maximize the total amount of mail delivered.

U.description: Duration of each trip taken by a runner (in hours)

C.description: The highest number of hours available in the village for deliveries.

V.description: The quantity of bags that a runner is permitted to transport during each journey.

N.description: The duration of time a canoeist spends on each trip (in hours)

Z.description: The quantity of bags that a person in a canoe can transport on every journey

E.description: The minimum amount of athletes required for participation.

P.description: The highest proportion of all deliveries that can be transported via canoe.

e.description: The quantity of individuals employed for transporting goods

p.description: Quantity of journeys taken by individuals using canoes

a.description: Quantity of journeys completed by individuals who run

constraints.description: Canoeers are limited to delivering only a portion, P, of the total mail that is to be delivered.
constraints.formulation: P \times \left( p \times Z + a \times V \right) \geq p \times Z

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(p * Z <= P * (a * V + p * Z))

constraints.description: The combined time devoted to deliveries by both runners and individuals using canoes must not surpass the limit of C hours.
A minimum of E runners are required to carry out the deliveries.

objective.description: Maximize the overall volume of mail transported by runners and canoeists while abiding by the specified time, capacity, and usage limitations.
